title: Invoica × PayAI Integration — Arb Bot Repurposing
audit_note: Arb bot code audited 2026-03-10 from Arb-bot-main/ in Invoica folder. Bug descriptions updated to reflect actual code state.

human_gate.action: PRE Sprint — PayAI Service Registration. Tarek must register 3 services at payai.network using investor relationship. Returns 3 ServiceIDs. See docs/plans/payai-integration-roadmap.md for exact steps.

tasks.title: PayAI Service Registration — Human Action
tasks.description: Register 3 Invoica services on PayAI partner portal. Use investor relationship for direct access. Get PAYAI_SERVICE_ID_ROUTER, PAYAI_SERVICE_ID_TREASURY, PAYAI_SERVICE_ID_ORACLE. Add to .env.example. Negotiate 50% fee reduction for first 3 months.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-01 CRITICAL: Rotate exposed private key + .gitignore
tasks.description: AUDITED: wallet/keypair.json contains a REAL 64-byte Solana private key committed to the repo (confirmed: [245,255,200,179,...]). This is a live key, not a placeholder. Three steps: 1) Add wallet/keypair.json to .gitignore immediately. 2) ROTATE THE KEY — generate new keypair, transfer any funds, never use the exposed key again. 3) Document key rotation in README. This must be the very first task — do not commit any other changes to this repo before the key is rotated.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-02: Replace CPMM quotes with DLMM swapQuote() for SOL legs
tasks.description: AUDITED: quotes.ts uses getAmountOut() (CPMM x*y=k formula) for SOL↔A and SOL↔B legs (lines 85-99). execute.ts correctly uses pool.swapQuote() for actual execution. This inconsistency means the quote engine shows phantom profit margins that don't match real execution prices — root cause of chasing false arbitrage. Fix: replace getAmountOut() calls in calculateCycle() and quoteTriangular() with DLMM.create()+swapQuote() calls, matching the execution path. The A↔B leg already uses quoteAB_DLMM() correctly — don't change that. Also check asol.ts and bsol.ts adapters which still export quoteCpmm() — mark deprecated but don't remove (smoke test utility).

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-03: Fix MeteoraPoolManager WSOL/WSOL mint placeholder
tasks.description: AUDITED: meteoraSDK.ts lines 24-27 — MeteoraPoolManager hardcodes WSOL ('So11111111111111111111111111111111111111112') as BOTH mint A and mint B in the mints object. This means every DLMM quote request passes WSOL as both input and output tokens, which is why all quotes fail. Fix: pass TOKEN_A_MINT.toBase58() and TOKEN_B_MINT.toBase58() from config.ts into the mints object. Two-line fix. Also: the getQuote() method signature passes swapYtoX to determine direction but mints must match the actual pool's token ordering — verify direction logic is consistent with the pool's baseMint/quoteMint.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-05: Fix BigInt() crash on base58 reserve fields (REST API path)
tasks.description: AUDITED: Both asol.ts (line 145) and bsol.ts (line 135) use raw BigInt(restData?.reserve_x ?? 0) at the REST API fallback path. A toBigInt() helper is defined in each file but NOT called here — only used elsewhere. If the REST API returns a base58 string in reserve_x or reserve_y, BigInt() throws SyntaxError and the whole fallback fails silently. Fix: replace the two BigInt() calls in each file with the existing toBigInt() helper. Also: the SDK path at asol.ts line 106 / bsol.ts line 95 uses BigInt(basics.activeBin.liqX) — if liqX is a BN object, this also crashes. Use toBigInt() there too.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-06 NEW: Add circuit breaker (consecutive loss pause)
tasks.description: AUDITED: bot.ts has rate limiting (MAX_TRADES_PER_MINUTE = 10) but NO consecutive-loss circuit breaker. The validation gate requires 'confirm circuit breaker triggers after 3 consecutive simulated losses' but there is nothing to trigger. Fix: add a consecutiveLosses counter in bot.ts. Increment on any tick where profitBps <= 0 or execution fails. Reset to 0 on any profitable tick. If consecutiveLosses >= 3, set circuitBreakerActive = true and stop executing (log only). Add a resetCircuitBreaker() export. Expose circuitBreakerActive in getBotStatus(). This is the safety rail required before the Treasury Manager can use this bot.

tasks.title: Arb Bot — F-07 NEW: Make full cycle execution atomic (Jito bundles)
tasks.description: AUDITED: bot.ts executeFullCycleTrade() executes each leg sequentially in a loop (lines 80-129). There is even a comment on line 76 acknowledging this: 'In production, you might want to use Jito bundles for atomic execution'. If leg 1 succeeds and leg 2 fails, the bot is left holding intermediate tokens with no recovery path — this is a real-money stranding risk. Fix: consolidate all leg instructions into a single Jito bundle using @jito-ts/sdk. If any leg's quote fails the slippage check between simulation and execution, the bundle fails atomically — nothing executes, no stranded tokens. Add simulate:true guard: Jito bundle dry-run before live submission. Note: this also addresses the probabilistic execution risk raised in the founder review (8-second window).

tasks.title: Arb Bot — Validation: 50 clean devnet cycles
tasks.description: Run 50 simulated cycles on devnet. 1) Confirm quotes are non-zero and directionally sane (SOL legs now use DLMM quotes, not CPMM). 2) Confirm ARB-003 fix: MeteoraPoolManager now returns valid quotes with real mints. 3) Confirm ARB-004 fix: REST API path does not crash on base58 reserve values. 4) Confirm circuit breaker (ARB-005): trigger 3 consecutive simulated losses — bot must pause and set circuitBreakerActive=true. 5) Confirm Jito bundle path (ARB-006): simulate a slippage breach — confirm bundle fails cleanly, no partial execution. DO NOT proceed to PAY-001 until all 5 checks pass.

tasks.title: Payment Router — payai-gate.ts middleware
tasks.description: Create middleware/payai-gate.ts. Uses @payai/x402-sdk verifyPayAI(). Applied to /payai/router/*, /payai/treasury/*, /payai/oracle/*. Returns HTTP 402 without valid PayAI x402 header. ServiceID from PAYAI_SERVICE_ID_ROUTER env var.

tasks.title: Payment Router — payment-router.ts service
tasks.description: Create backend/src/services/payment-router.ts. Wraps arb bot executeSwap() (now Jito-bundle-atomic) into route(invoiceId, tokenIn, amountIn) returning USDC settled. simulate:true flag by default. Uses the corrected arb bot after ARB-002 through ARB-006 fixes.

tasks.title: Payment Router — wire into settlement route
tasks.description: Update settlement route in backend/src/routes/ — call payment-router.ts before booking. If tokenIn is the settlement token, passthrough. Otherwise route via arb bot.

tasks.title: Treasury Manager — on-chain spending cap Solana program
tasks.description: FIRST TASK IN TREASURY SPRINT. Deploy a Solana program that enforces the per-cycle spending ceiling in on-chain program logic — not TypeScript. Program cannot release more than the cap regardless of what the yield skill instructs. On-chain state tracks cumulative daily loss; when threshold hit, program automatically freezes delegation for 24h. Auditable by anyone. One program audit required before mainnet exposure. See docs/plans/payai-integration-roadmap.md Risk Mitigations section for rationale.

tasks.title: Treasury Manager — gas vs yield model + minimum cycle floor
tasks.description: Model gas costs against yield using arb bot simulation. Solana tx ≈ $0.003; triangular arb = 3 txs ≈ $0.009. At 2% edge: minimum profitable cycle ≈ 1.5–2 USDC. Hard-code floor in treasury-yield.ts: skill does not trigger if projected gross yield < $0.015 (1.5× gas). Document the threshold and the model. Produces a signed-off number before TREAS-003 ships.

tasks.title: Treasury Manager — treasury-yield.ts OpenClaw skill with Jito bundles
tasks.description: Register invoica-treasury-yield skill via ClawRouter. Trigger: agent wallet above floor AND idle >10min AND above minimum cycle threshold (from TREAS-002). Calls arb bot fast-loop on Meteora BTC/SOL pool via Jito bundles (atomic). Deposits confirmed profit to agent wallet. Safety rails: max 2% drawdown/cycle, circuit breaker after 3 consecutive losses (uses ARB-005 circuitBreakerActive from bot.ts), minimum cycle floor from TREAS-002.

tasks.title: Treasury Manager — treasury-attribution.ts
tasks.description: Create backend/src/services/treasury-attribution.ts. Maps yield earned to agent identity. Primary data source for Reputation Oracle in REP-001.

tasks.title: Treasury Manager — validation gate
tasks.description: 1. Confirm minimum cycle size vs current Solana gas — verify $0.015 floor math with live numbers. 2. One agent completes idle yield cycle on devnet via Jito bundle path — confirm sim and execution in same block. 3. Force slippage breach mid-cycle — confirm bundle fails cleanly (no partial fill). 4. Force 3 consecutive losses — confirm circuit breaker from bot.ts pauses the skill. 5. Query on-chain program state (TREAS-001) — confirm daily counter increments and delegation freezes at threshold. 6. Non-PayAI request to /payai/treasury/* returns HTTP 402. All 6 must pass.

tasks.title: Reputation Oracle — acp-scorer.ts with 5-signal formula
tasks.description: Extend agent-reputation-dashboard (github.com/skingem1/agent-reputation-dashboard) to ingest treasury-attribution.ts output + PayAI retroactive transaction history. Build acp-scorer.ts: 5-signal weighted composite score — Signal 1: 37% yield (job-size weighted), Signal 2: 28% routing success, Signal 3: 18% settlement time, Signal 4: 7% completion rate, Signal 5: 10% verified dispute rate (5x negative multiplier on upheld disputes, 48h window). Score 0-1000. Cold start: agents with <10 PayAI transactions return UNRATED flag. Job-size weighting: 0.01 USDC task = 0.1 pts, 50 USDC job = 10 pts.

tasks.title: Reputation Oracle — oracle routes + dispute + endorsement
tasks.description: Build oracle.ts route: GET /payai/oracle/score/:agentId (returns score or UNRATED), GET /payai/oracle/leaderboard, POST /payai/oracle/record, POST /payai/oracle/dispute (triggers 48h window, applies 5x multiplier if upheld), POST /payai/oracle/endorse (binary satisfied signal from verified paying client only — no self-endorsement). Apply payai-gate.ts middleware using PAYAI_SERVICE_ID_ORACLE.

tasks.title: Reputation Oracle — stake-to-bid enforcement (jobs >20 USDC)
tasks.description: Jobs above 20 USDC require the accepting agent to post a 5 USDC stake via x402 before acceptance is confirmed. Stake is slashed if a verified dispute is upheld within 48h. Extends existing x402 payment mechanics. Agent with insufficient stake balance cannot accept jobs above threshold.

tasks.title: Marketplace — public treasury endpoint + frontend tab
tasks.description: Create backend/src/routes/treasury-public.ts (no auth). Add /treasury dashboard tab to Next.js frontend showing: yield generated, swarm total, per-agent P&L, circuit breaker status, emergency pause button, UNRATED vs scored agent count. Emergency pause kills yield engine only — payment routing remains live. Submit all 3 services to PayAI marketplace with cross-branding.

sprint_sequence: PRE-001 (human, 2 days) → parallel with Gas Backstop
ARB-001 FIRST — rotate exposed key before any other arb bot work
ARB-002, ARB-003, ARB-004, ARB-005 → parallel after ARB-001 (all CTO)
ARB-006 → after ARB-005 (Jito bundles depend on circuit breaker logic)
PAY-001, PAY-002, PAY-003 → Payment Router (Invoica repo, needs ARB-GATE + PRE-001)
TREAS-001 → on-chain Solana program FIRST (CTO, audit required before mainnet)
TREAS-002 → gas model, then TREAS-003 yield skill, then TREAS-004 attribution
REP-001 → 5-signal scorer, REP-002 + REP-003 parallel after REP-001

confirmed_bugs: F-01: Real Solana private key in wallet/keypair.json — confirmed byte array starting [245,255,200,179,...]
F-02: CPMM math in quotes.ts calculateCycle() lines 85-99 for SOL legs. execute.ts correctly uses pool.swapQuote() — inconsistency causes phantom profit signals
F-03: meteoraSDK.ts lines 24-27 — WSOL hardcoded as both mint A and mint B confirmed
F-05: asol.ts line 145 and bsol.ts line 135 use raw BigInt() at REST API path. toBigInt() helper defined but not called there
new_bugs: F-06: bot.ts executeFullCycleTrade() is sequential loop, not atomic. Line 76 comment acknowledges Jito bundles needed. Partial-fill risk if mid-cycle leg fails
F-07: No circuit breaker in bot.ts. Rate limiter (MAX_TRADES_PER_MINUTE=10) exists but no consecutive-loss pause. Validation gate assumes circuit breaker exists — it does not
already_ok: execute.ts uses pool.swapQuote() and pool.swap() correctly for actual execution
config.ts has safe PublicKey parsing with fallback handling
bsol.ts and asol.ts have comprehensive error handling and logging
bot.ts has rate limiting and trade size guards
bot.ts has simulateExecute flag working correctly

ceo_notes: PayAI integration does NOT displace Gas Backstop (Priority 1). Both tracks run in parallel. PRE-001 is human-only and should start immediately. ARB-001 (key rotation) is CRITICAL_SECURITY and must happen before any other arb bot work. The arb bot code is in Arb-bot-main/ in the Invoica folder. Two new bugs found in audit (F-06 non-atomic execution, F-07 missing circuit breaker) — both required before Treasury Manager can use the bot. On-chain Solana program (TREAS-001) requires audit before mainnet. Flag CTO when CEO Solana sprint ships.
